cards.back: ζ = 1/√2 ≈ 0.707. Gives maximally flat passband — no ripple.
H_B(s) = ω_n²/(s² + √2ω_ns + ω_n²). Note: √2ω_n = 2ζω_n when ζ = 1/√2.

cards.front: General Butterworth magnitude |H_B(ω)| for order N?

cards.back: s = −ω_n/√2 ± jω_n/√2. On circle of radius ω_n, at ±45° from negative real axis.
180°/N between adjacent poles, all on LHP semicircle of radius ω_c.

cards.front: N=3 Butterworth: how many poles and where?
cards.back: 3 poles: one on negative real axis (at −ω_c), plus one conjugate pair at ±60° from neg. real axis.
Replace s with s/ω_c in normalised H(s). Shifts cutoff from 1 rad/s to ω_c. |H| = 1/√(1+(ω/ω_c)^{2N}).
H₁ = 1/(s+1). H₂ = 1/(s²+√2s+1). Both with ω_c = 1 rad/s.
As many derivatives of |H(ω)|² as possible are zero at ω=0. No passband ripple. Flattest possible response.
Exactly at ω = ω_n (or ω_c). |H(ω_n)| = 1/√2 for every order N.
ζ ≥ 1/√2: monotonic LPF (no peak). ζ < 1/√2: resonance peak near ω_n (BPF-like behaviour).

cards.front: Frequency scaling example: 2nd order to ω_c = 1000?
cards.back: H = 10⁶/(s²+1414s+10⁶). Replace s→s/1000 in 1/(s²+√2s+1), multiply through by 10⁶.
